ladder.title: Ladder Game
ladder.description: Climb down the ladder and get your prize. A fair lottery game.
ladder.lead: Decide fair orders for your group. Who buys coffee? Who picks first?

howTo.heading: How to play
howTo.whatIsBody: The Ladder Game is a classic method for fairly deciding outcomes: who buys coffee, who picks first, who does the chore. You write participant names at the top and outcomes at the bottom, then follow a random ladder of vertical lines and horizontal rungs to determine each person's result. It's simple, fair, and fun—whether you're drawing lots for a team project, deciding chore order, or just having a good laugh.
howTo.howToBody: Start by selecting the number of players. Then enter each player's name and the possible outcomes (e.g., names vs. prizes or chores). If you leave outcomes blank, the game fills them in automatically. Click 'Build ladder' to generate a random ladder. The ladder is built fairly using computer randomness, ensuring every player has an equal 1/N chance of any outcome. Click on a player's name to reveal their result by tracing the path, or use 'Reveal all' to show everyone at once. You can reshuffle for a new ladder or reset to change players and outcomes.
howTo.featuresBody: Turn on 'Shuffle results' and the outcome slots are randomized so no one can predict the result in advance. Raise the 'Tension' and the ladder draws more horizontal rungs for a denser, more suspenseful trace—without changing fairness or the final result at all. 'Auto-suggest names' fills the board with fruit emoji and names for a quick start, while sound and confetti add to the fun of each reveal. Download the finished result as an image to share straight to a group chat. You can also play entirely by keyboard—number keys reveal a player, A reveals everyone, and R reshuffles.

useCases.heading: When the Ladder Game comes in handy
useCases.lead: From a simple bet to an awkward turn order, one round of the ladder settles it cleanly—no arguments afterward.

items.title: Coffee & lunch runs
items.body: Deciding who buys the coffee or covers lunch today. It's fairer than rock-paper-scissors, and because everyone watches the result unfold, no one grumbles about it later.

items.title: Chores & duties
items.body: Splitting up the dishes, recycling, or note-taking—the jobs no one volunteers for. Just enter the names and the duties, and it pairs them up at random.

items.title: Presentation & game order
items.body: Speaking order, karaoke turns, who goes first in a board game. When you need a full 1st-to-last ranking, switch to 'Ranking' mode to sort it all at once.

items.title: Making teams or groups
items.body: Picking sides or forming groups. Put the team names in the outcome slots and players get assigned to teams automatically.

items.title: Gift & prize exchanges
items.body: Secret Santa, ladder gifts, prize draws—any time you need to decide who gets what. Keep the outcomes hidden and reveal them one at a time to double the suspense.

items.q: Is the ladder game really fair?
items.a: Yes, absolutely. This digital ladder uses proven fair randomness—every player gets exactly 1/N probability of any outcome, regardless of starting position. Hand-drawn ladders often favor the center (a phenomenon called the 'ugly truth' in Korean), but this version eliminates that bias entirely.
You can play with 2 to 10 players.
No. Your result is completely independent of where you start. Enabling 'Hide results' before building the ladder makes it even more fair and prevents anyone from gaming the system.
Yes. Once all results are revealed, click 'Reshuffle' to generate a brand new ladder while keeping all the player names and outcomes.
Of course. Use the 'Copy results' button to copy the outcome mapping as text, then paste it into a message or email to share with your group.
Yes. The Ladder Game runs right in your mobile browser with nothing to install. If you have many players and the board gets wide, just scroll it left and right to see the whole ladder.

items.q: Are the names I type saved anywhere?
items.a: No. The player names and outcomes you enter are used only inside your browser and are never sent or stored anywhere. Refreshing the page clears them all, so you can use it without any privacy concerns.

items.q: Can I turn off the sound or animation?
items.a: Yes. Use 'Sound off' on the results panel to mute the effects. And if you enable 'reduce motion' in your device settings, the ladder-tracing animation and confetti are automatically minimized.

items.q: Can I share the result as an image?
items.a: Yes. Tap 'Download result' to save an image file showing every player and their outcome at a glance. Post it straight to a group chat or social media to share.

qna-a-day.title: One Question a Day
qna-a-day.description: Answer one daily question and keep a year-long personal reflection journal.

meta.title: One Question a Day · Daily Reflection
meta.description: A 365-day self-reflection journal tool. Answer the same question every day and compare your answers year over year. All entries are stored privately in your browser.

intro.title: One Question a Day
intro.lead: Spend a year answering the same question every day. On the same date each year, you'll see your past answer alongside your new one. Every entry stays private in your browser—a personal, safe space to reflect.

benefits.heading: What one line a day leaves behind

items.title: You can see yourself grow
items.body: Answer the same question a year later and changes you never noticed come into sharp focus. Seeing last year's you and today's you side by side is more honest than any self-help book.

items.title: Consistency without pressure
items.body: One question a day, a sentence or two—that's plenty. No need to write long, no need to never miss a day. Because it's a small habit, it lasts; and because it lasts, it becomes a record you'll treasure.

items.title: A space that's entirely yours
items.body: Every answer is stored only in this browser and uploaded nowhere. With no worry about who might see it, you can write down even the things you could never say out loud.

examples.heading: The kind of questions you'll meet
examples.lead: The 365 questions start light and reach all the way to what matters most. A few of the prompts waiting on the calendar:
items: What is your purpose in life?
What are you reading right now?
Who do you want to know better?
Is there any present you'd like from Santa Claus?
examples.note: The question changes automatically each day based on the date.

today.noPastYears: No entries yet for this date in past years

settings.resetConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all entries?
settings.resetConfirmBody: This action cannot be undone. Please export a backup first.
settings.privacyBody: All your answers are stored only in this browser and are never uploaded anywhere. If you switch devices or clear your browser data, your entries will be lost unless you export them first.
settings.storageUnavailable: This browser cannot save data — please export your entries as a backup
settings.quotaExceeded: Your browser's storage is full. Please export old entries and clear space.

howTo.heading: How to use One Question a Day
howTo.whatTitle: What is One Question a Day?
howTo.whatBody: One Question a Day is a year-long self-reflection journal. Every calendar date comes with one fixed, thoughtful question. You answer it once today, then next year on the same date, you'll see your old answer right alongside your new one. This creates a powerful 'then vs. now' mirror: you see exactly how you've changed, what you've learned, and what matters to you. All entries stay private in your browser.
howTo.howBody: The 'Today' tab shows today's question front and center with a text area to write your answer. It auto-saves as you type. Switch to the 'Calendar' tab to browse any date and edit past answers—or even write ahead for future dates. The 'Journal' tab lets you search your entire history and filter by year. Use the 'Settings' tab to back up your entries (download a JSON file), restore from a backup, or reset everything. That's it—simple and private.
howTo.whyTitle: Why answer the same question daily?
howTo.whyBody: Repeating the same question year after year teaches you about yourself at a deeper level. Your answer evolves: your priorities shift, your worldview grows, your understanding deepens. When you look back at what you wrote a year ago, you see your own progress made tangible. The habit builds slowly—one answer a day—but after a year, you've created a precious record of who you are and how you're becoming.

items.a: Entirely in this browser's local storage. Your answers never leave your device and are never uploaded anywhere. Because of this, if you clear your browser data or switch devices, you'll lose everything unless you export a backup first. We strongly recommend regular backups.

items.q: Can I move my entries to another device?
items.a: Yes, via export and import. On your current device, use 'Settings' → 'Export' to download a JSON backup file. On your new device, open One Question a Day, go to 'Settings' → 'Import', and select the file. Choose to merge (combine with any existing entries) or replace (start fresh with the imported entries).

items.q: Can I see what I wrote a year ago today?
items.a: Yes—that's the whole point. On the 'Today' tab, scroll down and you'll see a 'Past years' section showing all your previous answers for this calendar date, newest first.

items.q: What if I want to skip a day?
items.a: No problem. You don't have to answer every day. Come back whenever you want. You can also write ahead for future dates if you're feeling inspired.
The dataset has exactly 365 questions for 365 days. On February 29 (in leap years), you'll see the February 28 question—the same one non-leap years use.

Yes, if you clear your browser's cached data or storage. Since entries are only stored locally, they're vulnerable to the usual browser actions. That's why consistent backups are essential. With a backup file, you can always restore everything.
Your entries are private by default. No one can see them unless you intentionally share the export file. It's a personal space, just for you.
Once the page has loaded, writing and saving all happen inside your browser, so it keeps working even if your internet connection drops. Every answer is stored only on this device.

items.q: If I switch languages, do the questions change too?
items.a: Yes. There's a separate set of questions in Korean and English, so switching languages shows the same date's question in that language. Answers you've already written are kept intact.

meta.title: About Jurepi · Handy Online Tools
meta.description: Learn about Jurepi, a free online tool hub. No sign-up required, all tools work locally in your browser.

sections.title: What is Jurepi?
sections.body: Jurepi is a free online tool hub designed to make everyday tasks simpler. Whether you need to make a fair decision for your group, keep a daily reflection journal, or find quick answers to common problems, Jurepi has you covered. No sign-up, no login, no hassle—just open a tool and get started.

sections.title: Privacy first
sections.body: We don't collect your personal information. When you use Jurepi tools, your data stays on your device. Everything you enter—names in the ladder game, reflections in the journal, calculations—stays safely in your browser's local storage and never leaves your computer. Your privacy is our priority.

sections.title: Tools for every need
sections.body: We start with tools that solve real problems: a fair lottery game for making group decisions, and a daily journal for self-reflection. More tools are coming soon across categories like math, text, conversions, and fun. Check back often as we expand what Jurepi can do for you.

sections.title: Why is it free?
sections.body: Every Jurepi tool is, and will stay, free. We cover our running costs with the ads shown on the page. To keep those ads from getting in the way, we reserve their space and height in advance so the page never jumps around—and we never sell your personal information. You can turn ad and analytics cookies off or on anytime via 'Privacy settings' in the footer.

sections.title: Built in the open
sections.body: Jurepi is developed by a small team passionate about simplicity and quality. We share updates, ideas, and behind-the-scenes work on our blog. Follow along to learn about new tools, improvements, and how we think about design and engineering.

meta.title: Privacy Policy · Jurepi
meta.description: Learn how Jurepi handles your data, privacy, and the use of cookies and third-party services.

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Your privacy matters to us. This policy explains how Jurepi collects, uses, and protects your information.

sections.title: Information we collect
sections.body: We do not directly collect personal information like names, emails, or phone numbers. When you use Jurepi tools, any data you enter—such as participant names in the ladder game or journal entries in One Question a Day—is stored only in your browser's local storage. This data never leaves your device and is not sent to any server.

sections.title: Cookies and third-party services
sections.body: Jurepi uses Google AdSense and Google Analytics, which may use cookies to collect information about your browsing activity and help us understand how you use the site. These services have their own privacy policies. By using Jurepi, you consent to the collection and use of data by these third parties as described in their respective policies.

sections.title: Changing your privacy settings
sections.body: You can review and adjust your consent for cookies and analytics at any time. Look for 'Privacy settings' at the bottom of every page (in the footer). Clicking it will reopen the consent dialog, allowing you to enable or disable analytics and ads as you prefer.

sections.title: External links
sections.body: Jurepi may link to external websites and resources. We are not responsible for the privacy practices or content of these external sites. Please review their privacy policies before visiting.

sections.title: Children's privacy
sections.body: Jurepi is not intended for children under 13. We do not knowingly collect information from children under 13. If we become aware of such collection, we will take steps to delete the information immediately.

sections.title: Changes to this policy
sections.body: We may update this privacy policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or legal requirements. We will notify you of significant changes by updating the 'Last updated' date at the top of this policy.

sections.title: Contact us
sections.body: If you have questions or concerns about your privacy or how we handle your data, please contact us at dhan0213@naver.com. We aim to respond within a few days.

meta.title: Terms of Service · Jurepi
meta.description: Review the terms and conditions for using Jurepi and its tools.

terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using Jurepi, you agree to these terms. Please read them carefully.

sections.title: Agreement to terms
sections.body: By accessing and using Jurepi and its tools, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to any part of these terms, you may not use the site.

sections.title: Service provision
sections.body: Jurepi provides its tools and services on an 'as is' basis without warranties of any kind. We make no guarantee that the tools will always be available, function without errors, or meet your specific needs. We do not guarantee uptime, accuracy of results, or continuous operation.

sections.title: Acceptable use
sections.body: You agree to use Jurepi only for lawful purposes and in a way that does not infringe on the rights of others or restrict their use and enjoyment of the site. Prohibited behavior includes harassment, obtaining or attempting to obtain unauthorized access to systems or data, and any illegal activity.

sections.title: Limitation of liability
sections.body: To the fullest extent permitted by law, Jurepi and its creators shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising out of your use of or inability to use the tools, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages.

sections.title: Intellectual property
sections.body: All content on Jurepi, including text, graphics, logos, and code, is owned by or licensed to Jurepi and is protected by copyright and intellectual property laws. You may use the tools for personal, non-commercial purposes only.

sections.title: Third-party advertising and links
sections.body: Jurepi may display advertisements from Google AdSense and other advertisers. These ads are independent of Jurepi's content. External links on Jurepi are provided for convenience; Jurepi is not responsible for their content, accuracy, or policies. Your use of third-party services is at your own risk.

sections.title: Changes to terms
sections.body: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Changes become effective immediately upon posting. Your continued use of Jurepi following any changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated terms.

sections.title: Governing law and disputes
sections.body: These terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Korea, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any legal action or proceeding shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in South Korea.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: If you have questions or concerns regarding these terms, please contact us at dhan0213@naver.com.

meta.title: Contact · Jurepi
meta.description: Get in touch with the Jurepi team. We welcome feedback, bug reports, and partnership inquiries.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.intro: We love hearing from you! Have feedback, found a bug, or want to collaborate? Reach out anytime—we typically respond within a few days.
contact.note: Check our blog for updates on new tools, improvements, and behind-the-scenes stories.

consent.title: Analytics & Cookies
consent.body: Jurepi uses Google Analytics to collect anonymous usage statistics so we can improve your experience.
